text.title: Junior AI-Overreliance Coaching Guide Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns in coaching that destroy AI-fluency growth.

antipattern.symptom: Junior stops using AI after one harsh review; productivity drops, learning halts.
antipattern.root-cause: Reviewer used shame phrasing ('AI slop', 'just copy-pasted') instead of pattern-named feedback.
antipattern.fix: Use the 6-pattern vocabulary + delta-format. Frame as 'I see X signal here, what about Y?' not 'this is AI garbage'.

antipattern.symptom: Junior repeats the same overreliance pattern next PR despite previous fix.
antipattern.root-cause: Senior fixed it themselves without a teaching comment; no artefact for junior to learn from.
antipattern.fix: Either request changes with rationale, or accept with a teaching comment ('FYI here's why I'd write X instead').

antipattern.symptom: First overreliance PR escalated to manager; junior loses psych safety.
antipattern.root-cause: Reviewer skipped acknowledge/ask/block steps and jumped to escalation.
antipattern.fix: Apply the 4-step ladder (acknowledge → ask → block → escalate); escalation only after 3 blocked PRs.

antipattern.symptom: Junior gets 'this feels AI-generated' feedback with no actionable improvement target.
antipattern.root-cause: Reviewer lacks the 6-pattern vocabulary; gives vibes not signals.
antipattern.fix: Adopt the 6-signal taxonomy; each review comment names exactly one signal.
